<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // MODULE: Deep-Loop Executor Config
2: 
3: import { z } from 'zod';
4: 
5: // ───── TYPE DEFINITIONS ─────
6: 
7: export const EXECUTOR_KINDS = ['native', 'cli-codex', 'cli-claude-code', 'cli-opencode'] as const;
8: export type ExecutorKind = typeof EXECUTOR_KINDS[number];
9: 
10: export const REASONING_EFFORTS = ['none', 'minimal', 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'xhigh'] as const;
11: export type ReasoningEffort = typeof REASONING_EFFORTS[number];
12: 
13: export const SERVICE_TIERS = ['priority', 'standard', 'fast'] as const;
14: export type ServiceTier = typeof SERVICE_TIERS[number];
15: 
16: const SANDBOX_MODES = ['read-only', 'workspace-write', 'danger-full-access'] as const;
17: export type SandboxMode = typeof SANDBOX_MODES[number];
18: export type ClaudePermissionMode = 'plan' | 'acceptEdits' | 'bypassPermissions';
19: 
20: // ───── CONSTANTS ─────
21: 
22: export const executorConfigSchema = z.object({
23:   kind: z.enum(EXECUTOR_KINDS).default('native'),
24:   model: z.string().min(1).nullable().default(null),
25:   configDir: z.string().trim().min(1).nullable().default(null),
26:   reasoningEffort: z.enum(REASONING_EFFORTS).nullable().default(null),
27:   serviceTier: z.enum(SERVICE_TIERS).nullable().default(null),
28:   sandboxMode: z.enum(SANDBOX_MODES).nullable().default(null),
29:   timeoutSeconds: z.number().int().positive().default(900),
30:   // Optional fable-5 governor capsule for this executor's prompts. Universal and
31:   // kind-agnostic on purpose: intentionally absent from EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT
32:   // and the unsupported-field scan, so any executor kind may carry it. null = none.
33:   governor: z.string().min(1).nullable().default(null),
34: });
35: 
36: export type ExecutorConfig = z.infer<typeof executorConfigSchema>;
37: 
38: export const EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT: Record<ExecutorKind, readonly (keyof ExecutorConfig)[]> = {
39:   native: [],
40:   'cli-codex': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'serviceTier', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
41:   'cli-claude-code': ['model', 'configDir', 'reasoningEffort', 'sandboxMode', 'timeoutSeconds'],
42:   'cli-opencode': ['model', 'reasoningEffort', 'timeoutSeconds'],
43: };
44: 
45: // ───── DOMAIN ERRORS ─────
46: 
47: type ExecutorConfigIssue = {
48:   path: PropertyKey[];
49:   message: string;
50: };
51: 
52: export class ExecutorNotWiredError extends Error {
53:   kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-claude-code'>;
54: 
55:   constructor(kind: Extract<ExecutorKind, 'cli-claude-code'>) {
56:     super(`Executor kind '${kind}' is reserved in the schema but not yet wired. Awaiting future spec for ${kind} integration.`);
57:     this.name = 'ExecutorNotWiredError';
58:     this.kind = kind;
59:   }
60: }
61: 
62: export class ExecutorConfigError extends Error {
63:   issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[];
64: 
65:   constructor(input: { issues: ExecutorConfigIssue[] }) {
66:     super(input.issues.map((issue) => `${issue.path.map(String).join('.') || '<root>'}: ${issue.message}`).join('; '));
67:     this.name = 'ExecutorConfigError';
68:     this.issues = input.issues;
69:   }
70: }
71: 
72: // ───── HELPERS ─────
73: 
74: function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
75:   return value !== null && !Array.isArray(value) && typeof value === 'object';
76: }
77: 
78: function normalizeSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
79:   return mode ?? 'workspace-write';
80: }
81: 
82: function normalizeExecutorConfigInput(raw: unknown): unknown {
83:   if (!isRecord(raw) || !Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(raw, 'type')) {
84:     return raw;
85:   }
86: 
87:   const legacyKind = raw.type;
88:   if (typeof legacyKind !== 'string') {
89:     return raw;
90:   }
91: 
92:   if (typeof raw.kind === 'string' && raw.kind !== legacyKind) {
93:     throw new ExecutorConfigError({
94:       issues: [{
95:         path: ['type'],
96:         message: `deprecated executor field 'type' conflicts with canonical kind '${raw.kind}'`,
97:       }],
98:     });
99:   }
100: 
101:   console.warn("[executor-config] Deprecated executor field 'type' was provided; use 'kind' instead.");
102:   const { type: _legacyType, ...rest } = raw;
103:   return {
104:     ...rest,
105:     kind: typeof raw.kind === 'string' ? raw.kind : legacyKind,
106:   };
107: }
108: 
109: function normalizeIssues(error: z.ZodError<ExecutorConfig>): ExecutorConfigIssue[] {
110:   return error.issues.map((issue) => ({
111:     path: issue.path,
112:     message: issue.message,
113:   }));
114: }
115: 
116: // ───── EXPORTS ─────
117: 
118: /**
119:  * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Codex CLI sandbox mode.
120:  *
121:  * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
122:  * @returns Codex-compatible sandbox mode.
123:  */
124: export function resolveCodexSandboxMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): SandboxMode {
125:   return normalizeSandboxMode(mode);
126: }
127: 
128: /**
129:  * Map a generic sandbox mode to the Claude Code permission mode.
130:  *
131:  * @param mode - Generic sandbox mode or undefined.
132:  * @returns Claude-compatible permission mode.
133:  */
134: export function resolveClaudePermissionMode(mode: SandboxMode | null | undefined): ClaudePermissionMode {
135:   switch (normalizeSandboxMode(mode)) {
136:     case 'read-only':
137:       return 'plan';
138:     case 'danger-full-access':
139:       return 'bypassPermissions';
140:     default:
141:       return 'acceptEdits';
142:   }
143: }
144: 
145: /**
146:  * Parse and validate a raw executor configuration.
147:  *
148:  * Normalizes legacy `type` fields, validates against the Zod schema,
149:  * and enforces executor-kind-specific field support.
150:  *
151:  * @param raw - Raw input to parse (JSON-parsed object).
152:  * @returns Validated ExecutorConfig.
153:  * @throws {@link ExecutorConfigError} If validation fails.
154:  */
155: export function parseExecutorConfig(raw: unknown): ExecutorConfig {
156:   const parsed = executorConfigSchema.safeParse(normalizeExecutorConfigInput(raw));
157:   if (!parsed.success) {
158:     throw new ExecutorConfigError({
159:       issues: normalizeIssues(parsed.error),
160:     });
161:   }
162: 
163:   const config = parsed.data;
164:   if (config.kind === 'cli-codex' && config.model === null) {
165:     throw new ExecutorConfigError({
166:       issues: [{ path: ['model'], message: 'model is required when kind is cli-codex' }],
167:     });
168:   }
169:   const supportedFlags = EXECUTOR_KIND_FLAG_SUPPORT[config.kind];
170:   const unsupportedFields: string[] = [];
171:   const allOptionalFields: (keyof ExecutorConfig)[] = [
172:     'model',
173:     'configDir',
174:     'reasoningEffort',
175:     'serviceTier',
176:     'sandboxMode',
177:     'timeoutSeconds',
178:   ];
179: 
180:   for (const field of allOptionalFields) {
181:     if (!supportedFlags.includes(field) && config[field] !== null && !(field === 'timeoutSeconds' && config[field] === 900)) {
182:       unsupportedFields.push(field);
183:     }
184:   }
185: 
186:   if (unsupportedFields.length > 0) {
187:     throw new ExecutorConfigError({
188:       issues: unsupportedFields.map((field) => ({
189:         path: [field],
190:         message: `field '${field}' is not supported by executor kind '${config.kind}'. Supported fields for ${config.kind}: ${
191:           supportedFlags.length ? supportedFlags.join(', ') : 'none'
192:         }.`,
193:       })),
194:     });
195:   }
196: 
197:   return config;
198: }
199: 
200: /**
201:  * Resolve an executor configuration by merging CLI and file sources.
202:  *
203:  * @param sources - CLI and file partial configs (CLI takes precedence).
204:  * @returns Validated ExecutorConfig.
205:  */
206: export function resolveExecutorConfig(sources: {
207:   cli?: Partial<ExecutorConfig>;
208:   file?: Partial<ExecutorConfig>;
209: }): ExecutorConfig {
210:   const merged = {
211:     ...(sources.file ?? {}),
212:     ...(sources.cli ?? {}),
213:   };
214:   return parseExecutorConfig(merged);
215: }
216: 
217: // ───── FAN-OUT (MULTI-EXECUTOR) CONFIG ─────
218: //
219: // Opt-in layer ABOVE the single-executor path. When a fan-out config is present,
220: // the loop runs N executor "lineages" concurrently (capped), each running the
221: // existing sequential loop in its own isolated sub-packet. The single-executor
222: // path (executorConfigSchema / parseExecutorConfig) is untouched and remains the
223: // default; callers use EITHER a single `executor` OR a `fanout` block, never both.
224: 
225: const LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN = /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/;
226: 
227: /**
228:  * One fan-out lineage: a single executor config plus fan-out metadata.
229:  *
230:  * - `label`: directory-safe id for the lineage's isolated sub-packet.
231:  * - `count`: number of replicas of THIS config to run (e.g. 5x the same model).
232:  * - `iterations`: per-lineage max-iterations override; null = packet default.
233:  * - `promptFramework`: optional per-model prompt framing key (e.g. "costar",
234:  *   "tidd-ec") resolved against sk-prompt-small-model when a consumer renders the
235:  *   lineage prompt. null = consumer default. Loop-type-agnostic and optional, so
236:  *   research/review lineages that omit it are unaffected.
237:  */
238: export const lineageExecutorSchema = executorConfigSchema.extend({
239:   label: z.string().min(1).regex(LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN, {
240:     message: "label must match /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/ (lowercase, digits, hyphens; dir-safe)",
241:   }),
242:   count: z.number().int().positive().default(1),
243:   iterations: z.number().int().positive().nullable().default(null),
244:   promptFramework: z.string().min(1).nullable().default(null),
245: });
246: 
247: export type LineageExecutor = z.infer<typeof lineageExecutorSchema>;
248: 
249: export const fanoutConfigSchema = z.object({
250:   executors: z.array(lineageExecutorSchema).min(1),
251:   concurrency: z.number().int().positive().default(2),
252: });
253: 
254: export type FanoutConfig = z.infer<typeof fanoutConfigSchema>;
255: 
256: /**
257:  * Parse and validate a raw fan-out configuration.
258:  *
259:  * Each entry's executor subset is routed through {@link parseExecutorConfig} so
260:  * ALL existing kind/model/flag rules (cli-codex needs model, per-kind flag
261:  * support) apply per lineage with zero duplication.
262:  * Labels must be unique and base-label (count>1) expansion must not collide.
263:  *
264:  * @param raw - Raw input to parse (JSON-parsed object).
265:  * @returns Validated FanoutConfig.
266:  * @throws {@link ExecutorConfigError} If validation fails.
267:  */
268: export function parseFanoutConfig(raw: unknown): FanoutConfig {
269:   const parsed = fanoutConfigSchema.safeParse(raw);
270:   if (!parsed.success) {
271:     throw new ExecutorConfigError({ issues: parsed.error.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: issue.path, message: issue.message })) });
272:   }
273: 
274:   const config = parsed.data;
275: 
276:   // Reuse the canonical single-executor validator per entry (kind/model/flags).
277:   config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
278:     const { label: _label, count: _count, iterations: _iterations, promptFramework: _promptFramework, ...executorSubset } = entry;
279:     try {
280:       parseExecutorConfig(executorSubset);
281:     } catch (err) {
282:       if (err instanceof ExecutorConfigError) {
283:         throw new ExecutorConfigError({
284:           issues: err.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: ['executors', index, ...issue.path], message: issue.message })),
285:         });
286:       }
287:       throw err;
288:     }
289:   });
290: 
291:   // Labels must be unique among the declared lineages.
292:   const seenLabels = new Set<string>();
293:   config.executors.forEach((entry, index) => {
294:     if (seenLabels.has(entry.label)) {
295:       throw new ExecutorConfigError({
296:         issues: [{ path: ['executors', index, 'label'], message: `duplicate lineage label '${entry.label}'` }],
297:       });
298:     }
299:     seenLabels.add(entry.label);
300:   });
301: 
302:   // Expanded labels (count>1 → label-1, label-2, …) must not collide either.
303:   const expandedLabels = new Set<string>();
304:   for (const lineage of expandLineages(config)) {
305:     if (expandedLabels.has(lineage.label)) {
306:       throw new ExecutorConfigError({
307:         issues: [{ path: ['executors'], message: `expanded lineage label '${lineage.label}' collides; rename base labels` }],
308:       });
309:     }
310:     expandedLabels.add(lineage.label);
311:   }
312: 
313:   return config;
314: }
315: 
316: /**
317:  * Expand a fan-out config into concrete per-replica lineages.
318:  *
319:  * A `count` of 1 keeps the base label; `count > 1` yields `${label}-1` …
320:  * `${label}-N`. Each expanded lineage carries `count: 1` so downstream
321:  * consumers see one runnable unit per element.
322:  *
323:  * @param config - Validated FanoutConfig.
324:  * @returns Flat list of single-replica lineages (one runnable unit each).
325:  */
326: export function expandLineages(config: FanoutConfig): LineageExecutor[] {
327:   const lineages: LineageExecutor[] = [];
328:   for (const entry of config.executors) {
329:     if (entry.count === 1) {
330:       lineages.push({ ...entry, count: 1 });
331:       continue;
332:     }
333:     for (let replica = 1; replica <= entry.count; replica += 1) {
334:       lineages.push({ ...entry, label: `${entry.label}-${replica}`, count: 1 });
335:     }
336:   }
337:   return lineages;
338: }

(End of file - total 338 lines)

1: // MODULE: Deep-Loop Prompt Pack Renderer
2: 
3: import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs';
4: import { z } from 'zod';
5: 
6: // ───── TYPE DEFINITIONS ─────
7: 
8: export type PromptPackVariables = Record<string, string | number>;
9: 
10: // ───── CONSTANTS ─────
11: 
12: const PROMPT_PACK_TOKEN_PATTERN = /\{([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)\}/g;
13: const promptPackVariableNameSchema = z.string().regex(/^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$/);
14: 
15: // ───── DOMAIN ERRORS ─────
16: 
17: export class PromptPackError extends Error {
18:   missingVariables: string[];
19: 
20:   constructor(missingVariables: string[]) {
21:     super(`Missing prompt pack variables: ${missingVariables.join(', ')}`);
22:     this.name = 'PromptPackError';
23:     this.missingVariables = missingVariables;
24:   }
25: }
26: 
27: // ───── HELPERS ─────
28: 
29: function extractTemplateTokens(template: string): string[] {
30:   const tokens = new Set<string>();
31: 
32:   for (const match of template.matchAll(PROMPT_PACK_TOKEN_PATTERN)) {
33:     const tokenName = match[1];
34:     if (promptPackVariableNameSchema.safeParse(tokenName).success) {
35:       tokens.add(tokenName);
36:     }
37:   }
38: 
39:   return [...tokens];
40: }
41: 
42: // ───── EXPORTS ─────
43: 
44: /**
45:  * Render a prompt pack template by substituting variables.
46:  *
47:  * Reads the template file and replaces all `{variableName}` tokens
48:  * with values from the variables map.
49:  *
50:  * @param templatePath - Path to the prompt pack template file.
51:  * @param variables - Mapping of variable names to replacement values.
52:  * @returns Rendered template string with all variables substituted.
53:  * @throws {@link PromptPackError} If any required variables are missing.
54:  */
55: export function renderPromptPack(templatePath: string, variables: PromptPackVariables): string {
56:   const template = readFileSync(templatePath, 'utf8');
57:   const missingVariables = new Set<string>();
58: 
59:   const rendered = template.replace(PROMPT_PACK_TOKEN_PATTERN, (_, tokenName: string) => {
60:     if (!(tokenName in variables)) {
61:       missingVariables.add(tokenName);
62:       return `{${tokenName}}`;
63:     }
64: 
65:     return String(variables[tokenName]);
66:   });
67: 
68:   if (missingVariables.size > 0) {
69:     throw new PromptPackError([...missingVariables]);
70:   }
71: 
72:   return rendered;
73: }
74: 
75: /**
76:  * Validate that a prompt pack template contains expected variables.
77:  *
78:  * @param templatePath - Path to the prompt pack template file.
79:  * @param expectedVariables - Variable names that should be present.
80:  * @returns Report with present, missing, and extra variables.
81:  */
82: export function validatePromptPackTemplate(
83:   templatePath: string,
84:   expectedVariables: string[],
85: ): { present: string[]; missing: string[]; extra: string[] } {
86:   const template = readFileSync(templatePath, 'utf8');
87:   const templateTokens = extractTemplateTokens(template);
88:   const templateTokenSet = new Set(templateTokens);
89:   const expectedTokenSet = new Set(expectedVariables.filter((token) => promptPackVariableNameSchema.safeParse(token).success));
90: 
91:   return {
92:     present: expectedVariables.filter((token) => expectedTokenSet.has(token) && templateTokenSet.has(token)),
93:     missing: expectedVariables.filter((token) => expectedTokenSet.has(token) && !templateTokenSet.has(token)),
94:     extra: templateTokens.filter((token) => !expectedTokenSet.has(token)),
95:   };
96: }

(End of file - total 96 lines)
</content>

<content>
1: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
2: // ║ Council Multi-Seat Dispatch                                              ║
3: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
4: 
5: 'use strict';
6: 
7: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8: // 1. IMPORTS
9: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10: 
11: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12: // 2. CONSTANTS
13: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
14: 
15: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
16: // 3. HELPERS
17: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
18: 
19: function isRecord(value) {
20:   return value !== null && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value);
21: }
22: 
23: function normalizeSeat(seat, index) {
24:   if (typeof seat === 'string') {
25:     return { id: seat, input: { id: seat } };
26:   }
27:   if (!isRecord(seat)) {
28:     throw new TypeError(`seat at index ${index} must be a string or object`);
29:   }
30:   if (typeof seat.id !== 'string' || seat.id.trim() === '') {
31:     throw new TypeError(`seat at index ${index} must include a non-empty id`);
32:   }
33:   return { id: seat.id, input: seat };
34: }
35: 
36: function normalizeRoundId(roundId) {
37:   if (typeof roundId !== 'string' || roundId.trim() === '') {
38:     throw new TypeError('roundId must be a non-empty string');
39:   }
40:   return roundId;
41: }
42: 
43: function normalizeTimestamp(value) {
44:   if (value instanceof Date) {
45:     return value.toISOString();
46:   }
47:   if (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() !== '') {
48:     return value;
49:   }
50:   return new Date().toISOString();
51: }
52: 
53: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
54: // 4. CORE LOGIC
55: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
56: 
57: /**
58:  * Dispatch a single council seat and capture its result or error.
59:  *
60:  * Wraps the seat-specific dispatch function and records timing,
61:  * status (fulfilled/rejected), and any error details so the
62:  * multi-seat dispatcher can aggregate per-round results.
63:  *
64:  * @param {Object} params - Seat dispatch parameters.
65:  * @param {Object} params.seat - Normalized seat descriptor with id
66:  *   and input.
67:  * @param {number} params.index - Zero-based seat index within the round.
68:  * @param {string} params.roundId - Normalized round identifier.
69:  * @param {Function} params.dispatchSeat - Async function that accepts
70:  *   (seatInput, context) and returns the seat's output.
71:  * @param {Object} params.context - Arbitrary context forwarded to
72:  *   dispatchSeat.
73:  * @param {Function} params.now - Callable returning a Date or ISO
74:  *   timestamp for timing.
75:  * @returns {Promise<Object>} Per-seat result with seat_id, status,
76:  *   started_at_iso, completed_at_iso, duration_ms, and
77:  *   output or error.
78:  */
79: async function settleSeat({ seat, index, roundId, dispatchSeat, context, now }) {
80:   const startedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
81:   const startedAtMs = Date.now();
82:   try {
83:     const output = await dispatchSeat(seat.input, { roundId, seatIndex: index, context });
84:     return {
85:       seat_id: seat.id,
86:       status: 'fulfilled',
87:       started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
88:       completed_at_iso: normalizeTimestamp(now()),
89:       duration_ms: Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs),
90:       output,
91:     };
92:   } catch (error) {
93:     return {
94:       seat_id: seat.id,
95:       status: 'rejected',
96:       started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
97:       completed_at_iso: normalizeTimestamp(now()),
98:       duration_ms: Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs),
99:       error: {
100:         name: error && error.name ? String(error.name) : 'Error',
101:         message: error && error.message ? String(error.message) : String(error),
102:       },
103:     };
104:   }
105: }
106: 
107: /**
108:  * Dispatch all council seats for a single deliberation round in parallel.
109:  *
110:  * Normalizes inputs, dispatches each seat concurrently via
111:  * Promise.all, and returns an aggregated round result with per-seat
112:  * outcomes and a summary (total / succeeded / failed / all_failed).
113:  *
114:  * @param {Object} options - Dispatch configuration.
115:  * @param {string} options.roundId - Round identifier (non-empty string).
116:  * @param {Array<string|Object>} options.seats - Array of seat
117:  *   descriptors (string IDs or objects with id and input).
118:  * @param {Function} options.dispatchSeat - Async dispatch function
119:  *   invoked per seat.
120:  * @param {Object} [options.context={}] - Arbitrary context forwarded
121:  *   to each seat dispatch.
122:  * @param {Function} [options.now] - Callable returning a Date or ISO
123:  *   timestamp (default: () => new Date()).
124:  * @returns {Promise<Object>} Round result with round_id,
125:  *   started_at_iso, completed_at_iso, results array, and summary.
126:  * @throws {TypeError} If options is not an object, roundId is invalid,
127:  *   seats is empty, or dispatchSeat is not a function.
128:  */
129: async function dispatchCouncilSeats(options) {
130:   if (!isRecord(options)) {
131:     throw new TypeError('dispatchCouncilSeats options must be an object');
132:   }
133:   const roundId = normalizeRoundId(options.roundId);
134:   if (!Array.isArray(options.seats) || options.seats.length === 0) {
135:     throw new TypeError('seats must be a non-empty array');
136:   }
137:   if (typeof options.dispatchSeat !== 'function') {
138:     throw new TypeError('dispatchSeat must be a function');
139:   }
140: 
141:   const now = typeof options.now === 'function' ? options.now : () => new Date();
142:   const seats = options.seats.map(normalizeSeat);
143:   const startedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
144:   const results = await Promise.all(
145:     seats.map((seat, index) => settleSeat({
146:       seat,
147:       index,
148:       roundId,
149:       dispatchSeat: options.dispatchSeat,
150:       context: options.context || {},
151:       now,
152:     })),
153:   );
154:   const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
155:   const succeeded = results.filter((result) => result.status === 'fulfilled').length;
156:   const failed = results.length - succeeded;
157: 
158:   return {
159:     round_id: roundId,
160:     started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
161:     completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
162:     results,
163:     summary: {
164:       total: results.length,
165:       succeeded,
166:       failed,
167:       all_failed: failed === results.length,
168:     },
169:   };
170: }
171: 
172: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
173: // 5. EXPORTS
174: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
175: 
176: module.exports = {
177:   dispatchCouncilSeats,
178: };

(End of file - total 178 lines)
</content>

1: // MODULE: Coverage Graph Database
2: 
3: import Database from 'better-sqlite3';
4: import { mkdirSync, statSync } from 'node:fs';
5: import { dirname, join } from 'node:path';
6: import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
7: 
8: // ───── TYPE DEFINITIONS ─────
9: 
10: export type LoopType = 'research' | 'review' | 'context';
11: 
12: export type ResearchNodeKind = 'QUESTION' | 'FINDING' | 'CLAIM' | 'SOURCE';
13: 
14: export type ReviewNodeKind =
15:   | 'DIMENSION'
16:   | 'FILE'
17:   | 'FINDING'
18:   | 'EVIDENCE'
19:   | 'REMEDIATION'
20:   | 'BUG_CLASS'
21:   | 'INVARIANT'
22:   | 'PRODUCER'
23:   | 'CONSUMER'
24:   | 'TEST';
25: 
26: export type ContextNodeKind =
27:   | 'SLICE'
28:   | 'FILE'
29:   | 'SYMBOL'
30:   | 'PATTERN'
31:   | 'REUSE_CANDIDATE'
32:   | 'DEPENDENCY'
33:   | 'CONSTRAINT'
34:   | 'GAP';
35: 
36: export type NodeKind = ResearchNodeKind | ReviewNodeKind | ContextNodeKind;
37: 
38: export type ResearchRelation =
39:   | 'ANSWERS'
40:   | 'SUPPORTS'
41:   | 'CONTRADICTS'
42:   | 'SUPERSEDES'
43:   | 'DERIVED_FROM'
44:   | 'COVERS'
45:   | 'CITES';
46: 
47: export type ReviewRelation =
48:   | 'COVERS'
49:   | 'EVIDENCE_FOR'
50:   | 'CONTRADICTS'
51:   | 'RESOLVES'
52:   | 'CONFIRMS'
53:   | 'ESCALATES'
54:   | 'IN_DIMENSION'
55:   | 'IN_FILE';
56: 
57: export type ContextRelation =
58:   | 'CONTAINS'
59:   | 'REFERENCES'
60:   | 'IMPORTS'
61:   | 'DEPENDS_ON'
62:   | 'IMPLEMENTS'
63:   | 'EXPOSES'
64:   | 'REUSES'
65:   | 'CONSTRAINS'
66:   | 'COVERED_BY'
67:   | 'CONFIRMS'
68:   | 'CONTRADICTS';
69: 
70: export type Relation = ResearchRelation | ReviewRelation | ContextRelation;
71: 
72: export interface CoverageNode {
73:   id: string;
74:   specFolder: string;
75:   loopType: LoopType;
76:   sessionId: string;
77:   kind: NodeKind;
78:   name: string;
79:   contentHash?: string;
80:   iteration?: number;
81:   metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
82:   createdAt?: string;
83:   updatedAt?: string;
84: }
85: 
86: export interface CoverageEdge {
87:   id: string;
88:   specFolder: string;
89:   loopType: LoopType;
90:   sessionId: string;
91:   sourceId: string;
92:   targetId: string;
93:   relation: Relation;
94:   weight: number;
95:   metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
96:   createdAt?: string;
97: }
98: 
99: export interface CoverageSnapshot {
100:   id?: number;
101:   specFolder: string;
102:   loopType: LoopType;
103:   sessionId: string;
104:   iteration: number;
105:   metrics: Record<string, unknown>;
106:   nodeCount: number;
107:   edgeCount: number;
108:   createdAt?: string;
109: }
110: 
111: export interface Namespace {
112:   specFolder: string;
113:   loopType: LoopType;
114:   sessionId?: string;
115: }
116: 
117: // ───── CONSTANTS ─────
118: 
119: export const SCHEMA_VERSION = 3;
120: 
121: const DB_FILENAME = 'deep-loop-graph.sqlite';
122: export const COVERAGE_GRAPH_DATABASE_DIR = join(
123:   dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)),
124:   '..',
125:   '..',
126:   'database',
127: );
128: 
129: const MIN_WEIGHT = 0.0;
130: const MAX_WEIGHT = 2.0;
131: 
132: export const RESEARCH_WEIGHTS: Record<ResearchRelation, number> = {
133:   ANSWERS: 1.3,
134:   SUPPORTS: 1.0,
135:   CONTRADICTS: 0.8,
136:   SUPERSEDES: 1.5,
137:   DERIVED_FROM: 1.0,
138:   COVERS: 1.1,
139:   CITES: 1.0,
140: };
141: 
142: export const REVIEW_WEIGHTS: Record<ReviewRelation, number> = {
143:   COVERS: 1.3,
144:   EVIDENCE_FOR: 1.0,
145:   CONTRADICTS: 0.8,
146:   RESOLVES: 1.5,
147:   CONFIRMS: 1.0,
148:   ESCALATES: 1.2,
149:   IN_DIMENSION: 1.0,
150:   IN_FILE: 1.0,
151: };
152: 
153: // Context relation weights: REUSES is highest — surfacing existing code to extend
154: // is the primary value of context gathering (the repo "reuse, avoid new code" principle).
155: export const CONTEXT_WEIGHTS: Record<ContextRelation, number> = {
156:   CONTAINS: 1.0,
157:   REFERENCES: 1.0,
158:   IMPORTS: 1.2,
159:   DEPENDS_ON: 1.2,
160:   IMPLEMENTS: 1.4,
161:   EXPOSES: 1.3,
162:   REUSES: 1.5,
163:   CONSTRAINS: 1.1,
164:   COVERED_BY: 1.1,
165:   CONFIRMS: 1.0,
166:   CONTRADICTS: 0.8,
167: };
168: 
169: export const VALID_KINDS: Record<LoopType, readonly string[]> = {
170:   research: ['QUESTION', 'FINDING', 'CLAIM', 'SOURCE'] as const,
171:   review: [
172:     'DIMENSION',
173:     'FILE',
174:     'FINDING',
175:     'EVIDENCE',
176:     'REMEDIATION',
177:     'BUG_CLASS',
178:     'INVARIANT',
179:     'PRODUCER',
180:     'CONSUMER',
181:     'TEST',
182:   ] as const,
183:   context: [
184:     'SLICE',
185:     'FILE',
186:     'SYMBOL',
187:     'PATTERN',
188:     'REUSE_CANDIDATE',
189:     'DEPENDENCY',
190:     'CONSTRAINT',
191:     'GAP',
192:   ] as const,
193: };
194: 
195: export const VALID_RELATIONS: Record<LoopType, readonly string[]> = {
196:   research: ['ANSWERS', 'SUPPORTS', 'CONTRADICTS', 'SUPERSEDES', 'DERIVED_FROM', 'COVERS', 'CITES'] as const,
197:   review: ['COVERS', 'EVIDENCE_FOR', 'CONTRADICTS', 'RESOLVES', 'CONFIRMS', 'ESCALATES', 'IN_DIMENSION', 'IN_FILE'] as const,
198:   context: ['CONTAINS', 'REFERENCES', 'IMPORTS', 'DEPENDS_ON', 'IMPLEMENTS', 'EXPOSES', 'REUSES', 'CONSTRAINS', 'COVERED_BY', 'CONFIRMS', 'CONTRADICTS'] as const,
199: };
200: 
201: // ───── SCHEMA ─────
202: 
203: const SCHEMA_SQL = `
204:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS coverage_nodes (
205:     spec_folder TEXT NOT NULL,
206:     loop_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(loop_type IN ('research', 'review', 'context')),
207:     session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
208:     id TEXT NOT NULL,
209:     kind TEXT NOT NULL,
210:     name TEXT NOT NULL,
211:     content_hash TEXT,
212:     iteration INTEGER,
213:     metadata TEXT,
214:     created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
215:     updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
216:     PRIMARY KEY (spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id)
217:   );
218: 
219:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS coverage_edges (
220:     spec_folder TEXT NOT NULL,
221:     loop_type TEXT NOT NULL,
222:     session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
223:     id TEXT NOT NULL,
224:     source_id TEXT NOT NULL,
225:     target_id TEXT NOT NULL,
226:     relation TEXT NOT NULL,
227:     weight REAL DEFAULT 1.0 CHECK(weight >= 0.0 AND weight <= 2.0),
228:     metadata TEXT,
229:     created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
230:     CHECK(source_id != target_id),
231:     PRIMARY KEY (spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id),
232:     FOREIGN KEY (spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, source_id)
233:       REFERENCES coverage_nodes (spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id),
234:     FOREIGN KEY (spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, target_id)
235:       REFERENCES coverage_nodes (spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id)
236:   );
237: 
238:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS coverage_snapshots (
239:     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
240:     spec_folder TEXT NOT NULL,
241:     loop_type TEXT NOT NULL,
242:     session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
243:     iteration INTEGER NOT NULL,
244:     metrics TEXT,
245:     node_count INTEGER,
246:     edge_count INTEGER,
247:     created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
248:     UNIQUE(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, iteration)
249:   );
250: 
251:   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
252:     version INTEGER NOT NULL
253:   );
254: 
255:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_folder_type ON coverage_nodes(spec_folder, loop_type);
256:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_kind ON coverage_nodes(kind);
257:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_session ON coverage_nodes(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id);
258:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_iteration ON coverage_nodes(iteration);
259:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_source ON coverage_edges(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, source_id);
260:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_target ON coverage_edges(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, target_id);
261:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_relation ON coverage_edges(relation);
262:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_folder_type ON coverage_edges(spec_folder, loop_type);
263:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_session ON coverage_edges(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id);
264:   CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_coverage_snapshot_session ON coverage_snapshots(session_id);
265: `;
266: 
267: // ───── DATABASE LIFECYCLE ─────
268: 
269: let db: Database.Database | null = null;
270: let dbPath: string | null = null;
271: let statementDb: Database.Database | null = null;
272: const preparedStatements = new Map<string, Database.Statement>();
273: 
274: /**
275:  * Initialize (or get) the coverage graph database.
276:  *
277:  * Creates the database directory and file, applies schema migrations,
278:  * and enables WAL journaling and foreign keys.
279:  *
280:  * @param dbDir - Directory for the database file.
281:  * @returns The initialized Database instance.
282:  */
283: export function initDb(dbDir: string): Database.Database {
284:   if (db) return db;
285: 
286:   try {
287:     mkdirSync(dbDir, { recursive: true });
288:     dbPath = join(dbDir, DB_FILENAME);
289:     db = new Database(dbPath);
290:     db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
291:     db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');
292: 
293:     const schemaTableExists = db.prepare(
294:       "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='schema_version'",
295:     ).get() as { name: string } | undefined;
296:     if (schemaTableExists) {
297:       const existing = db.prepare('SELECT version FROM schema_version LIMIT 1').get() as { version: number } | undefined;
298:       if (existing && existing.version < SCHEMA_VERSION) {
299:         db.exec(`
300:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_folder_type;
301:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_kind;
302:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_session;
303:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_iteration;
304:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_source;
305:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_target;
306:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_relation;
307:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_folder_type;
308:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_edge_session;
309:           DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_coverage_snapshot_session;
310:           DROP TABLE IF EXISTS coverage_edges;
311:           DROP TABLE IF EXISTS coverage_nodes;
312:           DROP TABLE IF EXISTS coverage_snapshots;
313:         `);
314:       }
315:     }
316: 
317:     db.exec(SCHEMA_SQL);
318: 
319:     const versionRow = db.prepare('SELECT version FROM schema_version LIMIT 1').get() as { version: number } | undefined;
320:     if (!versionRow) {
321:       db.prepare('INSERT INTO schema_version (version) VALUES (?)').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
322:     } else if (versionRow.version < SCHEMA_VERSION) {
323:       db.prepare('UPDATE schema_version SET version = ?').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
324:     }
325: 
326:     return db;
327:   } catch (err: unknown) {
328:     if (db) {
329:       try { db.close(); } catch { }
330:     }
331:     db = null;
332:     dbPath = null;
333:     throw err;
334:   }
335: }
336: 
337: /**
338:  * Get the current database instance (lazy-initializes if needed).
339:  *
340:  * @returns The Database instance.
341:  */
342: export function getDb(): Database.Database {
343:   if (!db) initDb(COVERAGE_GRAPH_DATABASE_DIR);
344:   return db!;
345: }
346: 
347: /**
348:  * Close the database connection and clear cached state.
349:  */
350: export function closeDb(): void {
351:   if (db) {
352:     db.close();
353:     db = null;
354:     dbPath = null;
355:     statementDb = null;
356:     preparedStatements.clear();
357:   }
358: }
359: 
360: // ───── HELPERS ─────
361: 
362: function prepareStatement(sql: string): Database.Statement {
363:   const currentDb = getDb();
364:   if (statementDb !== currentDb) {
365:     preparedStatements.clear();
366:     statementDb = currentDb;
367:   }
368: 
369:   let statement = preparedStatements.get(sql);
370:   if (!statement) {
371:     statement = currentDb.prepare(sql);
372:     preparedStatements.set(sql, statement);
373:   }
374:   return statement;
375: }
376: 
377: function buildNamespaceWhere(ns: Namespace): { clause: string; params: unknown[] } {
378:   const parts: string[] = ['spec_folder = ?', 'loop_type = ?'];
379:   const params: unknown[] = [ns.specFolder, ns.loopType];
380:   if (ns.sessionId) {
381:     parts.push('session_id = ?');
382:     params.push(ns.sessionId);
383:   }
384:   return { clause: parts.join(' AND '), params };
385: }
386: 
387: function rowToNode(r: Record<string, unknown>): CoverageNode {
388:   return {
389:     id: r.id as string,
390:     specFolder: r.spec_folder as string,
391:     loopType: r.loop_type as LoopType,
392:     sessionId: r.session_id as string,
393:     kind: r.kind as NodeKind,
394:     name: r.name as string,
395:     contentHash: r.content_hash as string | undefined,
396:     iteration: r.iteration as number | undefined,
397:     metadata: r.metadata ? JSON.parse(r.metadata as string) : undefined,
398:     createdAt: r.created_at as string | undefined,
399:     updatedAt: r.updated_at as string | undefined,
400:   };
401: }
402: 
403: function rowToEdge(r: Record<string, unknown>): CoverageEdge {
404:   return {
405:     id: r.id as string,
406:     specFolder: r.spec_folder as string,
407:     loopType: r.loop_type as LoopType,
408:     sessionId: r.session_id as string,
409:     sourceId: r.source_id as string,
410:     targetId: r.target_id as string,
411:     relation: r.relation as Relation,
412:     weight: r.weight as number,
413:     metadata: r.metadata ? JSON.parse(r.metadata as string) : undefined,
414:     createdAt: r.created_at as string | undefined,
415:   };
416: }
417: 
418: function rowToSnapshot(r: Record<string, unknown>): CoverageSnapshot {
419:   return {
420:     id: r.id as number,
421:     specFolder: r.spec_folder as string,
422:     loopType: r.loop_type as LoopType,
423:     sessionId: r.session_id as string,
424:     iteration: r.iteration as number,
425:     metrics: r.metrics ? JSON.parse(r.metrics as string) : {},
426:     nodeCount: r.node_count as number,
427:     edgeCount: r.edge_count as number,
428:     createdAt: r.created_at as string | undefined,
429:   };
430: }
431: 
432: // ───── EXPORTS ─────
433: 
434: /**
435:  * Clamp weight to valid range [0.0, 2.0].
436:  *
437:  * @param weight - Raw weight value.
438:  * @returns Clamped weight in [0.0, 2.0]. Non-finite values default to 1.0.
439:  */
440: export function clampWeight(weight: number): number {
441:   if (!Number.isFinite(weight)) return 1.0;
442:   return Math.max(MIN_WEIGHT, Math.min(MAX_WEIGHT, weight));
443: }
444: 
445: /**
446:  * Insert or update a node scoped to the full namespace.
447:  *
448:  * Every existence check is namespace-qualified so two sessions reusing
449:  * the same logical id cannot collide.
450:  *
451:  * @param node - Coverage node to upsert.
452:  * @returns The node ID.
453:  */
454: export function upsertNode(node: CoverageNode): string {
455:   const d = getDb();
456:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
457:   const metadataStr = node.metadata ? JSON.stringify(node.metadata) : null;
458: 
459:   const existing = prepareStatement(
460:     'SELECT id FROM coverage_nodes WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ? AND id = ?',
461:   ).get(node.specFolder, node.loopType, node.sessionId, node.id) as { id: string } | undefined;
462:   if (existing) {
463:     prepareStatement(`
464:       UPDATE coverage_nodes SET
465:         kind = ?, name = ?, content_hash = ?, iteration = ?,
466:         metadata = ?, updated_at = ?
467:       WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ? AND id = ?
468:     `).run(
469:       node.kind, node.name, node.contentHash ?? null, node.iteration ?? null,
470:       metadataStr, now,
471:       node.specFolder, node.loopType, node.sessionId, node.id,
472:     );
473:     return node.id;
474:   }
475: 
476:   prepareStatement(`
477:     INSERT INTO coverage_nodes (
478:       spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id, kind, name,
479:       content_hash, iteration, metadata, created_at, updated_at
480:     )
481:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
482:   `).run(
483:     node.specFolder, node.loopType, node.sessionId, node.id,
484:     node.kind, node.name, node.contentHash ?? null,
485:     node.iteration ?? null, metadataStr, now, now,
486:   );
487:   return node.id;
488: }
489: 
490: /**
491:  * Get a node by ID inside a namespace.
492:  *
493:  * @param ns - Namespace for scoping.
494:  * @param id - Node ID.
495:  * @returns The node or null if not found (or if sessionId is missing).
496:  */
497: export function getNode(ns: Namespace, id: string): CoverageNode | null {
498:   if (!ns.sessionId) return null;
499:   const d = getDb();
500:   const row = prepareStatement(
501:     'SELECT * FROM coverage_nodes WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ? AND id = ?',
502:   ).get(ns.specFolder, ns.loopType, ns.sessionId, id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
503:   return row ? rowToNode(row) : null;
504: }
505: 
506: /**
507:  * Get all nodes in a namespace.
508:  *
509:  * @param ns - Namespace for scoping.
510:  * @returns Array of coverage nodes.
511:  */
512: export function getNodes(ns: Namespace): CoverageNode[] {
513:   const d = getDb();
514:   const { clause, params } = buildNamespaceWhere(ns);
515:   const rows = prepareStatement(`SELECT * FROM coverage_nodes WHERE ${clause}`).all(...params) as Record<string, unknown>[];
516:   return rows.map(rowToNode);
517: }
518: 
519: /**
520:  * Get nodes of a specific kind in a namespace.
521:  *
522:  * @param ns - Namespace for scoping.
523:  * @param kind - Node kind to filter by.
524:  * @returns Array of matching coverage nodes.
525:  */
526: export function getNodesByKind(ns: Namespace, kind: NodeKind): CoverageNode[] {
527:   const d = getDb();
528:   const { clause, params } = buildNamespaceWhere(ns);
529:   const rows = prepareStatement(`SELECT * FROM coverage_nodes WHERE ${clause} AND kind = ?`).all(...params, kind) as Record<string, unknown>[];
530:   return rows.map(rowToNode);
531: }
532: 
533: /**
534:  * Delete a node and its connected edges inside a namespace.
535:  *
536:  * @param ns - Namespace for scoping.
537:  * @param id - Node ID to delete.
538:  * @returns True if a node was deleted.
539:  */
540: export function deleteNode(ns: Namespace, id: string): boolean {
541:   if (!ns.sessionId) return false;
542:   const d = getDb();
543:   const tx = d.transaction(() => {
544:     prepareStatement(
545:       'DELETE FROM coverage_edges WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ? AND (source_id = ? OR target_id = ?)',
546:     ).run(ns.specFolder, ns.loopType, ns.sessionId, id, id);
547:     const result = prepareStatement(
548:       'DELETE FROM coverage_nodes WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ? AND id = ?',
549:     ).run(ns.specFolder, ns.loopType, ns.sessionId, id);
550:     return result.changes > 0;
551:   });
552:   return tx();
553: }
554: 
555: /**
556:  * Insert or update an edge scoped to the full namespace.
557:  *
558:  * Rejects self-loops and clamps weights. Two sessions that both emit
559:  * an edge with the same logical id get independent rows.
560:  *
561:  * @param edge - Coverage edge to upsert.
562:  * @returns The edge ID or null if rejected (self-loop).
563:  */
564: export function upsertEdge(edge: CoverageEdge): string | null {
565:   if (edge.sourceId === edge.targetId) {
566:     return null;
567:   }
568: 
569:   const d = getDb();
570:   const weight = clampWeight(edge.weight);
571:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
572:   const metadataStr = edge.metadata ? JSON.stringify(edge.metadata) : null;
573: 
574:   const existing = prepareStatement(
575:     'SELECT id FROM coverage_edges WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ? AND id = ?',
576:   ).get(edge.specFolder, edge.loopType, edge.sessionId, edge.id) as { id: string } | undefined;
577:   if (existing) {
578:     prepareStatement(`
579:       UPDATE coverage_edges SET
580:         relation = ?, weight = ?, metadata = ?
581:       WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ? AND id = ?
582:     `).run(
583:       edge.relation, weight, metadataStr,
584:       edge.specFolder, edge.loopType, edge.sessionId, edge.id,
585:     );
586:     return edge.id;
587:   }
588: 
589:   prepareStatement(`
590:     INSERT INTO coverage_edges (
591:       spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, id, source_id, target_id,
592:       relation, weight, metadata, created_at
593:     )
594:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
595:   `).run(
596:     edge.specFolder, edge.loopType, edge.sessionId, edge.id,
597:     edge.sourceId, edge.targetId,
598:     edge.relation, weight, metadataStr, now,
599:   );
600:   return edge.id;
601: }
602: 
603: /**
604:  * Get an edge by ID inside a namespace.
605:  *
606:  * @param ns - Namespace for scoping.
607:  * @param id - Edge ID.
608:  * @returns The edge or null if not found (or if sessionId is missing).
609:  */
610: export function getEdge(ns: Namespace, id: string): CoverageEdge | null {
611:   if (!ns.sessionId) return null;
612:   const d = getDb();
613:   const row = prepareStatement(
614:     'SELECT * FROM coverage_edges WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ? AND id = ?',
615:   ).get(ns.specFolder, ns.loopType, ns.sessionId, id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
616:   return row ? rowToEdge(row) : null;
617: }
618: 
619: /**
620:  * Get all edges in a namespace.
621:  *
622:  * @param ns - Namespace for scoping.
623:  * @returns Array of coverage edges.
624:  */
625: export function getEdges(ns: Namespace): CoverageEdge[] {
626:   const d = getDb();
627:   const { clause, params } = buildNamespaceWhere(ns);
628:   const rows = prepareStatement(`SELECT * FROM coverage_edges WHERE ${clause}`).all(...params) as Record<string, unknown>[];
629:   return rows.map(rowToEdge);
630: }
631: 
632: /**
633:  * Get edges from a source node inside a namespace.
634:  *
635:  * @param ns - Namespace for scoping.
636:  * @param sourceId - Source node ID.
637:  * @returns Array of outgoing edges.
638:  */
639: export function getEdgesFrom(ns: Namespace, sourceId: string): CoverageEdge[] {
640:   if (!ns.sessionId) return [];
641:   const d = getDb();
642:   const rows = prepareStatement(
643:     'SELECT * FROM coverage_edges WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ? AND source_id = ?',
644:   ).all(ns.specFolder, ns.loopType, ns.sessionId, sourceId) as Record<string, unknown>[];
645:   return rows.map(rowToEdge);
646: }
647: 
648: /**
649:  * Get edges to a target node inside a namespace.
650:  *
651:  * @param ns - Namespace for scoping.
652:  * @param targetId - Target node ID.
653:  * @returns Array of incoming edges.
654:  */
655: export function getEdgesTo(ns: Namespace, targetId: string): CoverageEdge[] {
656:   if (!ns.sessionId) return [];
657:   const d = getDb();
658:   const rows = prepareStatement(
659:     'SELECT * FROM coverage_edges WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ? AND target_id = ?',
660:   ).all(ns.specFolder, ns.loopType, ns.sessionId, targetId) as Record<string, unknown>[];
661:   return rows.map(rowToEdge);
662: }
663: 
664: /**
665:  * Update an edge's weight and/or metadata inside a namespace.
666:  *
667:  * @param ns - Namespace for scoping.
668:  * @param id - Edge ID to update.
669:  * @param updates - New weight and/or metadata.
670:  * @returns True if the edge was found and updated.
671:  */
672: export function updateEdge(
673:   ns: Namespace,
674:   id: string,
675:   updates: { weight?: number; metadata?: Record<string, unknown> },
676: ): boolean {
677:   if (!ns.sessionId) return false;
678:   const d = getDb();
679:   const existing = prepareStatement(
680:     'SELECT * FROM coverage_edges WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ? AND id = ?',
681:   ).get(ns.specFolder, ns.loopType, ns.sessionId, id) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
682:   if (!existing) return false;
683: 
684:   const weight = updates.weight !== undefined ? clampWeight(updates.weight) : existing.weight as number;
685:   const metadataStr = updates.metadata ? JSON.stringify(updates.metadata) : existing.metadata as string | null;
686: 
687:   prepareStatement(
688:     'UPDATE coverage_edges SET weight = ?, metadata = ? WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ? AND id = ?',
689:   ).run(weight, metadataStr, ns.specFolder, ns.loopType, ns.sessionId, id);
690:   return true;
691: }
692: 
693: /**
694:  * Delete an edge by ID inside a namespace.
695:  *
696:  * @param ns - Namespace for scoping.
697:  * @param id - Edge ID to delete.
698:  * @returns True if the edge was deleted.
699:  */
700: export function deleteEdge(ns: Namespace, id: string): boolean {
701:   if (!ns.sessionId) return false;
702:   const d = getDb();
703:   const result = prepareStatement(
704:     'DELETE FROM coverage_edges WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ? AND id = ?',
705:   ).run(ns.specFolder, ns.loopType, ns.sessionId, id);
706:   return result.changes > 0;
707: }
708: 
709: /**
710:  * Create a coverage snapshot for a given iteration.
711:  *
712:  * @param snapshot - Snapshot data to persist.
713:  * @returns The auto-incremented snapshot ID.
714:  */
715: export function createSnapshot(snapshot: CoverageSnapshot): number {
716:   const d = getDb();
717:   const metricsStr = JSON.stringify(snapshot.metrics);
718: 
719:   const result = prepareStatement(`
720:     INSERT INTO coverage_snapshots (
721:       spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, iteration, metrics, node_count, edge_count
722:     )
723:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
724:     ON CONFLICT(spec_folder, loop_type, session_id, iteration) DO UPDATE SET
725:       metrics = excluded.metrics,
726:       node_count = excluded.node_count,
727:       edge_count = excluded.edge_count
728:   `).run(
729:     snapshot.specFolder, snapshot.loopType, snapshot.sessionId, snapshot.iteration,
730:     metricsStr, snapshot.nodeCount, snapshot.edgeCount,
731:   );
732:   return Number(result.lastInsertRowid);
733: }
734: 
735: /**
736:  * Get the latest snapshot for a namespace.
737:  *
738:  * @param specFolder - Spec folder for scoping.
739:  * @param loopType - Loop type ('research' or 'review').
740:  * @param sessionId - Optional session ID for scoping.
741:  * @returns The latest snapshot or null if none found.
742:  */
743: export function getLatestSnapshot(specFolder: string, loopType: LoopType, sessionId?: string): CoverageSnapshot | null {
744:   const d = getDb();
745:   if (sessionId) {
746:     const row = prepareStatement(`
747:       SELECT * FROM coverage_snapshots
748:       WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ?
749:       ORDER BY iteration DESC LIMIT 1
750:     `).get(specFolder, loopType, sessionId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
751:     return row ? rowToSnapshot(row) : null;
752:   }
753:   const row = prepareStatement(`
754:     SELECT * FROM coverage_snapshots
755:     WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ?
756:     ORDER BY iteration DESC LIMIT 1
757:   `).get(specFolder, loopType) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
758:   return row ? rowToSnapshot(row) : null;
759: }
760: 
761: /**
762:  * Get all snapshots for a namespace (ordered by iteration).
763:  *
764:  * @param specFolder - Spec folder for scoping.
765:  * @param loopType - Loop type ('research' or 'review').
766:  * @param sessionId - Optional session ID for scoping.
767:  * @returns Array of snapshots in ascending iteration order.
768:  */
769: export function getSnapshots(specFolder: string, loopType: LoopType, sessionId?: string): CoverageSnapshot[] {
770:   const d = getDb();
771:   if (sessionId) {
772:     const rows = prepareStatement(`
773:       SELECT * FROM coverage_snapshots
774:       WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? AND session_id = ?
775:       ORDER BY iteration ASC
776:     `).all(specFolder, loopType, sessionId) as Record<string, unknown>[];
777:     return rows.map(rowToSnapshot);
778:   }
779:   const rows = prepareStatement(`
780:     SELECT * FROM coverage_snapshots
781:     WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ?
782:     ORDER BY iteration ASC
783:   `).all(specFolder, loopType) as Record<string, unknown>[];
784:   return rows.map(rowToSnapshot);
785: }
786: 
787: /**
788:  * Get graph statistics for a namespace.
789:  *
790:  * @param specFolder - Spec folder for scoping.
791:  * @param loopType - Loop type ('research' or 'review').
792:  * @returns Statistics including node/edge counts, breakdowns, and schema info.
793:  */
794: export function getStats(specFolder: string, loopType: LoopType): {
795:   totalNodes: number;
796:   totalEdges: number;
797:   nodesByKind: Record<string, number>;
798:   edgesByRelation: Record<string, number>;
799:   lastIteration: number | null;
800:   schemaVersion: number;
801:   dbFileSize: number | null;
802: } {
803:   const d = getDb();
804: 
805:   const totalNodes = (prepareStatement(
806:     'SELECT COUNT(*) as c FROM coverage_nodes WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ?',
807:   ).get(specFolder, loopType) as { c: number }).c;
808: 
809:   const totalEdges = (prepareStatement(
810:     'SELECT COUNT(*) as c FROM coverage_edges WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ?',
811:   ).get(specFolder, loopType) as { c: number }).c;
812: 
813:   const nodesByKind: Record<string, number> = {};
814:   const kindRows = prepareStatement(
815:     'SELECT kind, COUNT(*) as c FROM coverage_nodes WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? GROUP BY kind',
816:   ).all(specFolder, loopType) as { kind: string; c: number }[];
817:   for (const r of kindRows) nodesByKind[r.kind] = r.c;
818: 
819:   const edgesByRelation: Record<string, number> = {};
820:   const relRows = prepareStatement(
821:     'SELECT relation, COUNT(*) as c FROM coverage_edges WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ? GROUP BY relation',
822:   ).all(specFolder, loopType) as { relation: string; c: number }[];
823:   for (const r of relRows) edgesByRelation[r.relation] = r.c;
824: 
825:   const lastIterRow = prepareStatement(
826:     'SELECT MAX(iteration) as max_iter FROM coverage_snapshots WHERE spec_folder = ? AND loop_type = ?',
827:   ).get(specFolder, loopType) as { max_iter: number | null } | undefined;
828:   const lastIteration = lastIterRow?.max_iter ?? null;
829: 
830:   let dbFileSize: number | null = null;
831:   if (dbPath) {
832:     try { dbFileSize = statSync(dbPath).size; } catch { }
833:   }
834: 
835:   return {
836:     totalNodes,
837:     totalEdges,
838:     nodesByKind,
839:     edgesByRelation,
840:     lastIteration,
841:     schemaVersion: SCHEMA_VERSION,
842:     dbFileSize,
843:   };
844: }
845: 
846: /**
847:  * Batch upsert nodes and edges in a single transaction.
848:  *
849:  * @param nodes - Nodes to upsert.
850:  * @param edges - Edges to upsert.
851:  * @returns Counts of inserted nodes, inserted edges, and rejected edges.
852:  */
853: export function batchUpsert(
854:   nodes: CoverageNode[],
855:   edges: CoverageEdge[],
856: ): { insertedNodes: number; insertedEdges: number; rejectedEdges: number } {
857:   const d = getDb();
858:   let insertedNodes = 0;
859:   let insertedEdges = 0;
860:   let rejectedEdges = 0;
861: 
862:   const tx = d.transaction(() => {
863:     for (const node of nodes) {
864:       upsertNode(node);
865:       insertedNodes++;
866:     }
867:     for (const edge of edges) {
868:       const result = upsertEdge(edge);
869:       if (result) {
870:         insertedEdges++;
871:       } else {
872:         rejectedEdges++;
873:       }
874:     }
875:   });
876:   tx();
877: 
878:   return { insertedNodes, insertedEdges, rejectedEdges };
879: }
