51:       threshold: number;
52:       language: VerificationLanguage;
53:       details: string;
54:       stages: VerificationStageScores;
55:     };
56: 
57: export type PostDispatchAdvisory = { code: string; detail: string; fieldPath?: string };
58: 
59: type PostDispatchFailureReason =
60:   | 'iteration_file_missing'
61:   | 'iteration_file_empty'
62:   | 'jsonl_not_appended'
63:   | 'jsonl_missing_fields'
64:   | 'jsonl_parse_error'
65:   | 'jsonl_wrong_type'
66:   | 'delta_file_missing'
67:   | 'delta_file_empty'
68:   | 'delta_file_missing_iteration_record'
69:   | 'executor_missing'
70:   | 'dispatch_failure_logged'
71:   | 'verification_degraded'
72:   | 'v2_missing_ledger'
73:   | 'v2_uncited_ledger_row'
74:   | 'v2_broken_linked_finding'
75:   | 'v2_shallow_finding_details'
76:   | 'delta_iteration_id_mismatch';
77: 
78: export type PostDispatchValidateResult =
79:   | { ok: true; warnings?: PostDispatchAdvisory[] }
80:   | {
81:       ok: false;
82:       reason: PostDispatchFailureReason;
83:       details: string;
84:       warnings?: PostDispatchAdvisory[];
85:     };
86: 
87: // ───── DOMAIN ERRORS ─────
88: 
89: export class PostDispatchValidationError extends Error {
90:   result: PostDispatchValidateResult;
91: 
92:   constructor(result: PostDispatchValidateResult) {
93:     super(result.ok ? 'Post-dispatch validation unexpectedly succeeded' : `${result.reason}: ${result.details}`);
94:     this.name = 'PostDispatchValidationError';
95:     this.result = result;
96:   }
97: }
98: 
99: // ───── CONSTANTS ─────
100: 
101: const CANONICAL_ITERATION_TYPE = 'iteration' as const;
102: const DEFAULT_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD = 0.5;
103: const SUPPORTED_VERIFICATION_LANGUAGES = new Set<VerificationLanguage>([
104:   'python',
105:   'typescript',
106:   'javascript',
107:   'rust',
108:   'go',
109: ]);
110: const REVIEW_ITERATION_FIELDS = [
111:   'type',
112:   'iteration',
113:   'mode',
114:   'run',
115:   'status',
116:   'focus',
117:   'dimensions',
118:   'filesReviewed',
119:   'findingsCount',
120:   'findingsSummary',
121:   'findingsNew',
122:   'findingDetails',
123:   'newFindingsRatio',
124:   'sessionId',
125:   'generation',
126:   'lineageMode',
127:   'timestamp',
128:   'durationMs',
129: ] as const;
130: const V2_SCOPE_CLASSES = new Set(['trivial', 'standard', 'complex']);
131: const V2_ENFORCEMENT_MODES = new Set(['strict', 'warn', 'skip']);
132: const V2_GRAPH_COVERAGE_MODES = new Set(['graph', 'graphless_fallback', 'unavailable_blocked']);
133: 
134: type V2EnforcementMode = 'warn' | 'strict' | 'off';
135: type V2ValidationFailure = {
136:   reason: PostDispatchFailureReason;
137:   details: string;
138:   fieldPath?: string;
139: };
140: 
141: // ───── HELPERS ─────
142: 
143: function getLastNonEmptyLine(content: string): string | null {
144:   const lines = content.split(/\r?\n/);
145: 
146:   for (let index = lines.length - 1; index >= 0; index -= 1) {
147:     const line = lines[index];
148:     if (line.trim() !== '') {
149:       return line;
150:     }
151:   }
152: 
153:   return null;
154: }
155: 
156: function isObjectRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
157:   return value !== null && !Array.isArray(value) && typeof value === 'object';
158: }
159: 
160: function isStringArray(value: unknown): value is string[] {
161:   return Array.isArray(value) && value.every((entry) => typeof entry === 'string');
162: }
163: 
164: function isNonEmptyStringArray(value: unknown): value is string[] {
165:   return isStringArray(value) && value.length > 0;
166: }
167: 
168: function isVerificationLanguage(value: string): value is VerificationLanguage {
169:   return SUPPORTED_VERIFICATION_LANGUAGES.has(value as VerificationLanguage);
170: }
171: 
172: function normalizeFenceLanguage(language: string): VerificationLanguage | null {
173:   const normalized = language.trim().toLowerCase();
174:   if (normalized === 'py' || normalized === 'python') return 'python';
175:   if (normalized === 'ts' || normalized === 'tsx' || normalized === 'typescript') return 'typescript';
176:   if (normalized === 'js' || normalized === 'mjs' || normalized === 'javascript') return 'javascript';
177:   if (normalized === 'rs' || normalized === 'rust') return 'rust';
178:   if (normalized === 'go' || normalized === 'golang') return 'go';
179:   return null;
180: }
181: 
182: function extractCodeBlocks(
183:   content: string,
184:   allowedLanguages: VerificationLanguage[],
185: ): Array<{ language: VerificationLanguage; code: string }> {
186:   const blocks: Array<{ language: VerificationLanguage; code: string }> = [];
187:   const fencePattern = /```([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)\n([\s\S]*?)```/g;
188:   let match: RegExpExecArray | null;
189: 
190:   while ((match = fencePattern.exec(content)) !== null) {
191:     const language = normalizeFenceLanguage(match[1] ?? '');
192:     if (language && (allowedLanguages.length === 0 || allowedLanguages.includes(language))) {
193:       blocks.push({ language, code: match[2] ?? '' });
194:     }
195:   }
196: 
197:   return blocks;
198: }
199: 
200: function bracesBalanced(code: string): boolean {
201:   let balance = 0;
202:   for (const char of code) {
203:     if (char === '{') balance += 1;
204:     if (char === '}') balance -= 1;
205:     if (balance < 0) return false;
206:   }
207:   return balance === 0;
208: }
209: 
210: function hasPlaceholderOrTruncation(code: string): boolean {
211:   return /TODO|NotImplementedError|pass\s+#\s*placeholder|\[\.\.\. truncated|\.\.\. rest of|\.\.\. more/i.test(code);
212: }
213: 
214: function hasObviousRuntimeFailure(code: string): boolean {
215:   return /throw new Error|raise\s+\w*Error|panic!\s*\(|process\.exit\s*\(\s*1\s*\)|Deno\.exit\s*\(\s*1\s*\)/.test(code);
216: }
217: 
218: function hasSyntaxShape(language: VerificationLanguage, code: string): boolean {
219:   if (code.trim() === '') return false;
220:   if (hasPlaceholderOrTruncation(code)) return false;
221:   if (language === 'javascript' || language === 'typescript' || language === 'rust' || language === 'go') {
222:     return bracesBalanced(code);
223:   }
224:   if (language === 'python') {
225:     return !/^\s*(def|class|if|for|while|try|with)\b[^\n:]*$/m.test(code);
226:   }
227:   return true;
228: }
229: 
230: function scoreCodeBlock(language: VerificationLanguage, code: string): VerificationStageScores {
231:   const compiled = hasSyntaxShape(language, code);
232:   const executed = compiled && !hasObviousRuntimeFailure(code);
233:   const testsPassed = executed && !/assert\s+false|expect\([^)]*\)\.toBeFalsy\(\)|FAIL:/i.test(code);
234:   const lintClean = compiled && !/[ \t]+$/m.test(code) && !hasPlaceholderOrTruncation(code);
235: 
236:   return {
237:     compiled,
238:     executed,
239:     testsPassed,
240:     lintClean,
241:     autoFixed: false,
242:   };
243: }
244: 
245: function buildVerificationDegradedEvent(input: {
246:   confidence: number;
247:   threshold: number;
248:   language: VerificationLanguage;
249:   details: string;
250: }): string {
251:   return JSON.stringify({
252:     type: 'event',
253:     event: 'verification_degraded',
254:     status: 'degraded',
255:     confidence: input.confidence,
256:     threshold: input.threshold,
257:     language: input.language,
258:     reason: 'verification_degraded',
259:     detail: input.details,
260:     timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
261:   });
262: }
263: 
264: function getV2EnforcementMode(): V2EnforcementMode {
265:   const raw = process.env.DEEP_REVIEW_V2_ENFORCEMENT?.trim().toLowerCase();
266:   if (raw === 'strict' || raw === 'off' || raw === 'warn') {
267:     return raw;
268:   }
269:   return 'warn';
270: }
271: 
272: // The evidence contract defaults to advisory: malformed metadata warns, absent
273: // metadata is silent, and only an explicit strict opt-in turns it into a hard
274: // failure. This mirrors the v2 off/warn/strict pattern so operators tune it the
275: // same way; the default keeps every legacy exchange passing.
276: function getEvidenceEnforcementMode(): V2EnforcementMode {
277:   const raw = process.env.DEEP_LOOP_EVIDENCE_ENFORCEMENT?.trim().toLowerCase();
278:   if (raw === 'strict' || raw === 'off' || raw === 'warn') {
279:     return raw;
280:   }
281:   return 'warn';
282: }
283: 
284: // Turn an evidence-contract validation into advisory warnings. Absent and
285: // well-formed payloads produce nothing; a malformed payload produces one
286: // advisory per offending field path. Never throws, never blocks.
287: function evidenceAdvisories(record: Record<string, unknown>): PostDispatchAdvisory[] {
288:   const validation = validateEvidenceContract(record.evidence);
289:   if (validation.status !== 'malformed') {
290:     return [];
291:   }
292:   return validation.issues.map((issue) => ({
293:     code: 'evidence_contract_malformed',
294:     detail: issue.detail,
295:     fieldPath: `evidence.${issue.fieldPath}`,
296:   }));
297: }
298: 
299: function isLegacyNonTrivialReviewRecord(record: Record<string, unknown>): boolean {
300:   return (Array.isArray(record.findingDetails) && record.findingDetails.length > 0)
301:     || (Array.isArray(record.dimensions) && record.dimensions.length > 1);
302: }
303: 
304: function v2Failure(
305:   reason: PostDispatchFailureReason,
306:   details: string,
307:   fieldPath?: string,
308: ): V2ValidationFailure {
309:   return { reason, details, fieldPath };
310: }
311: 
312: function warningsFromV2Failures(failures: V2ValidationFailure[]): PostDispatchAdvisory[] {
313:   return failures.map((failure) => ({
314:     code: `warn_${failure.reason}`,
315:     detail: failure.details,
316:     fieldPath: failure.fieldPath,
317:   }));
318: }
319: 
320: function findLastIterationRecord(content: string): Record<string, unknown> | null {
321:   const lines = content.split(/\r?\n/);
322:   for (let index = lines.length - 1; index >= 0; index -= 1) {
323:     const line = lines[index]?.trim();
324:     if (!line) continue;
325:     try {
326:       const parsed = JSON.parse(line);
327:       if (isObjectRecord(parsed) && parsed.type === CANONICAL_ITERATION_TYPE) {
328:         return parsed;
329:       }
330:     } catch {
331:     }
332:   }
333:   return null;
334: }
335: 
336: function validateV2IterationRecord(
337:   record: Record<string, unknown>,
338:   deltaIterationRecord: Record<string, unknown> | null,
339: ): V2ValidationFailure[] {
340:   const failures: V2ValidationFailure[] = [];
341:   const applicability = record.reviewDepthApplicability;
342:   const targetSelection = record.targetSelection;
343:   const searchCoverage = record.searchCoverage;
344: 
345:   if (!isObjectRecord(applicability)) {
346:     failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'reviewDepthApplicability must be an object', 'reviewDepthApplicability'));
347:   } else {
348:     if (!V2_SCOPE_CLASSES.has(String(applicability.scopeClass))) {
349:       failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'reviewDepthApplicability.scopeClass must be trivial, standard, or complex', 'reviewDepthApplicability.scopeClass'));
350:     }
351:     if (!V2_ENFORCEMENT_MODES.has(String(applicability.enforcement))) {
352:       failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'reviewDepthApplicability.enforcement must be strict, warn, or skip', 'reviewDepthApplicability.enforcement'));
353:     }
354:   }
355: 
356:   if (!isObjectRecord(targetSelection)) {
357:     failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'targetSelection must be an object', 'targetSelection'));
358:   } else {
359:     if (!Array.isArray(targetSelection.selectedTargets)) {
360:       failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'targetSelection.selectedTargets must be an array', 'targetSelection.selectedTargets'));
361:     }
362:     if (!Array.isArray(targetSelection.discoveryMethods)) {
363:       failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'targetSelection.discoveryMethods must be an array', 'targetSelection.discoveryMethods'));
364:     }
365:   }
366: 
367:   if (!isObjectRecord(searchCoverage)) {
368:     failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'searchCoverage must be an object', 'searchCoverage'));
369:   } else {
370:     if (!V2_GRAPH_COVERAGE_MODES.has(String(searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode))) {
371:       failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', 'searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode must be graph, graphless_fallback, or unavailable_blocked', 'searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode'));
372:     }
373:   }
374: 
375:   const scopeClass = isObjectRecord(applicability) && typeof applicability.scopeClass === 'string'
376:     ? applicability.scopeClass
377:     : '';
378:   const enforcement = isObjectRecord(applicability) && typeof applicability.enforcement === 'string'
379:     ? applicability.enforcement
380:     : '';
381:   const nonTrivialScope = scopeClass !== 'trivial' || enforcement !== 'skip';
382: 
383:   if (nonTrivialScope) {
384:     if (!Array.isArray(record.searchLedger) || record.searchLedger.length === 0) {
385:       failures.push(v2Failure('v2_missing_ledger', 'non-trivial v2 records must include at least one searchLedger row', 'searchLedger'));
386:     }
387: 
388:     const findingIds = new Set(
389:       Array.isArray(record.findingDetails)
390:         ? record.findingDetails
391:             .filter(isObjectRecord)
392:             .map((finding) => (typeof finding.id === 'string' ? finding.id : null))
393:             .filter((id): id is string => id !== null)
394:         : [],
395:     );
396: 
397:     if (Array.isArray(record.searchLedger)) {
398:       for (let index = 0; index < record.searchLedger.length; index += 1) {
399:         const row = record.searchLedger[index];
400:         if (!isObjectRecord(row)) {
401:           failures.push(v2Failure('jsonl_missing_fields', `searchLedger[${index}] must be an object`, `searchLedger[${index}]`));
402:           continue;
403:         }
404:         const actions = Array.isArray(row.searchActions) ? row.searchActions : [];
405:         if (
406:           actions.length === 0
407:           || actions.some((action) => !isObjectRecord(action) || !isNonEmptyStringArray(action.evidenceRefs))
408:         ) {
409:           failures.push(v2Failure('v2_uncited_ledger_row', `searchLedger[${index}] must cite evidenceRefs on every searchActions entry`, `searchLedger[${index}].searchActions`));
410:         }
411:         if (row.disposition === 'finding') {
412:           const linkedFindingId = typeof row.linkedFindingId === 'string' ? row.linkedFindingId : '';
413:           if (!linkedFindingId || !findingIds.has(linkedFindingId)) {
414:             failures.push(v2Failure('v2_broken_linked_finding', `searchLedger[${index}] linkedFindingId '${linkedFindingId || '[missing]'}' is not present in findingDetails[].id`, `searchLedger[${index}].linkedFindingId`));
415:           }
416:         }
417:       }
418:     }
419: 
420:     if (Array.isArray(record.findingDetails)) {
421:       for (let index = 0; index < record.findingDetails.length; index += 1) {
422:         const finding = record.findingDetails[index];
423:         if (!isObjectRecord(finding)) continue;
424:         const disposition = typeof finding.disposition === 'string' ? finding.disposition : 'active';
425:         if (disposition !== 'active') continue;
426:         if (!isNonEmptyStringArray(finding.affectedSurfaceHints) || typeof finding.scopeProof !== 'string' || finding.scopeProof.trim() === '') {
427:           const id = typeof finding.id === 'string' ? finding.id : `index ${index}`;
428:           failures.push(v2Failure('v2_shallow_finding_details', `active finding ${id} must include non-empty scopeProof and affectedSurfaceHints`, `findingDetails[${index}]`));
429:         }
430:       }
431:     }
432:   }
433: 
434:   if (deltaIterationRecord) {
435:     const stateIteration = typeof record.iteration === 'number' ? record.iteration : record.run;
436:     const deltaIteration = typeof deltaIterationRecord.iteration === 'number'
437:       ? deltaIterationRecord.iteration
438:       : deltaIterationRecord.run;
439:     if (typeof stateIteration === 'number' && typeof deltaIteration === 'number' && stateIteration !== deltaIteration) {
440:       failures.push(v2Failure(
441:         'delta_iteration_id_mismatch',
442:         `state-log iteration ${stateIteration} does not match delta iteration ${deltaIteration}`,
443:         'iteration',
444:       ));
445:     }
446:   }
447: 
448:   return failures;
449: }
450: 
451: // ───── EXPORTS ─────
452: 
453: /**
454:  * Compute a verification confidence score from stage scores.
455:  *
456:  * Weighted scoring: compiled (0.35), executed (0.25), testsPassed (0.25),
457:  * lintClean (0.1), minus autoFixed penalty (0.05).
458:  *
459:  * @param stages - Verification stage scores.
460:  * @returns Confidence score in [0.0, 1.0].
461:  */
462: export function computeVerificationConfidence(stages: VerificationStageScores): number {
463:   let score = 0;
464:   if (stages.compiled) score += 0.35;
465:   if (stages.executed) score += 0.25;
466:   if (stages.testsPassed) score += 0.25;
467:   if (stages.lintClean) score += 0.1;
468:   if (stages.autoFixed) score -= 0.05;
469:   return Math.max(0, Math.min(1, Number(score.toFixed(4))));
470: }
471: 
472: /**
473:  * Run optional verification pass on code blocks extracted from the iteration file.
474:  *
475:  * Scans the iteration file for fenced code blocks in configured languages
476:  * and scores their quality against the threshold.
477:  *
478:  * @param iterationFile - Path to the iteration output file.
479:  * @param recipeConfig - Verification recipe configuration.
480:  * @returns Pass result with confidence, threshold, and language info.
481:  */
482: export function runOptionalVerificationPass(
483:   iterationFile: string,
484:   recipeConfig?: PostDispatchRecipeConfig,
485: ): VerificationPassResult {
486:   if (recipeConfig?.verification_enabled !== true) {
487:     return { ok: true, skipped: true, reason: 'verification_disabled' };
488:   }
489: 
490:   const configuredLanguages = (recipeConfig.verification_languages ?? []).filter(isVerificationLanguage);
491:   const threshold = recipeConfig.verification_threshold ?? DEFAULT_VERIFICATION_THRESHOLD;
492:   const content = readFileSync(iterationFile, 'utf8');
493:   const codeBlocks = extractCodeBlocks(content, configuredLanguages);
494: 
495:   if (codeBlocks.length === 0) {
496:     return { ok: true, skipped: true, reason: 'no_code_output' };
497:   }
498: 
499:   const scoredBlocks = codeBlocks.map((block) => {
500:     const stages = scoreCodeBlock(block.language, block.code);
501:     return {
502:       language: block.language,
503:       confidence: computeVerificationConfidence(stages),
504:       stages,
505:     };
506:   });
507:   const lowest = scoredBlocks.reduce((currentLowest, candidate) =>
508:     candidate.confidence < currentLowest.confidence ? candidate : currentLowest,
509:   );
510: 
511:   if (lowest.confidence < threshold) {
512:     return {
513:       ok: false,
514:       reason: 'verification_degraded',
515:       confidence: lowest.confidence,
516:       threshold,
517:       language: lowest.language,
518:       details: `verification confidence ${lowest.confidence.toFixed(2)} below threshold ${threshold.toFixed(2)}`,
519:       stages: lowest.stages,
520:     };
521:   }
522: 
523:   return {
524:     ok: true,
525:     skipped: false,
526:     confidence: lowest.confidence,
527:     threshold,
528:     language: lowest.language,
529:     stages: lowest.stages,
530:   };
531: }
532: 
533: /**
534:  * Validate post-dispatch iteration outputs.
535:  *
536:  * Verifies that the JSONL state log was appended, the iteration file exists
537:  * and is non-empty, required fields are present, and optional v2 depth checks
538:  * and code verification passes are applied.
539:  *
540:  * @param input - Validation input with paths, field requirements, and config.
541:  * @returns Validation result indicating pass/fail with details.
542:  */
543: // Heuristic behavioral signals for an iteration's prose — drift nudges toward the
544: // fable-5 signature (result-first openers, lean hedging, evidence-backed claims).
545: // These are advisory only: callers see them in `warnings`, never in the `ok` verdict.
546: function computeBehavioralAdvisories(text: string): PostDispatchAdvisory[] {
547:   const out: PostDispatchAdvisory[] = [];
548:   const firstLine = (text.split('\n').find((l) => l.trim() && !l.trim().startsWith('#')) || '').trim();
549:   if (/^(?:I'?ll\b|I will\b|Let me\b|Let's\b|I'?m going to\b|Now I\b|First,?\s+I\b|I need to\b)/i.test(firstLine)) {
550:     out.push({ code: 'behavioral_self_opener', detail: 'iteration opens with self-narration; prefer a result-first opener' });
551:   }
552:   const caveats = (text.match(/\b(?:however|that said|worth noting|keep in mind|bear in mind|on the other hand|to be fair)\b/gi) || []).length;
553:   if (caveats >= 4) {
554:     out.push({ code: 'behavioral_high_caveat', detail: `${caveats} hedging caveats; keep only load-bearing qualifiers` });
555:   }
556:   let claims = 0;
557:   let backed = 0;
558:   for (const sentence of text.split(/(?<=[.!?])\s+/)) {
559:     if (/\b(?:done|completed?|verified|passes|passing|works|confirmed|shipped)\b/i.test(sentence)) {
560:       claims += 1;
561:       if (/(?:\[SOURCE:|`[^`]+`|\b[\w./-]+\.(?:ts|js|cjs|py|sh|md|json|yaml)\b|:\d+\b|\bvalidate\.sh\b|\bvitest\b|\bPASS)/.test(sentence)) backed += 1;
562:     }
563:   }
564:   if (claims >= 2 && backed / claims < 0.5) {
565:     out.push({ code: 'behavioral_uncited_completion', detail: `${claims - backed}/${claims} completion claims lack nearby evidence` });
566:   }
567:   return out;
568: }
569: 
570: export function validateIterationOutputs(input: PostDispatchValidateInput): PostDispatchValidateResult {
571:   const warnings: PostDispatchAdvisory[] = [];
572: 
573:   repairJsonlTail(input.stateLogPath);
574: 
575:   if (statSync(input.stateLogPath).size <= input.previousStateLogSize) {
576:     return {
577:       ok: false,
578:       reason: 'jsonl_not_appended',
579:       details: `no new records since ${input.previousStateLogSize} bytes`,
580:     };
581:   }
582: 
583:   const stateLogContent = readFileSync(input.stateLogPath, 'utf8');
584:   const lastLine = getLastNonEmptyLine(stateLogContent);
585: 
586:   try {
587:     const parsedRecord = JSON.parse(lastLine ?? '');
588:     if (!isObjectRecord(parsedRecord)) {
589:       return {
590:         ok: false,
591:         reason: 'jsonl_parse_error',
592:         details: 'Last JSONL line is not an object',
593:       };
594:     }
595: 
596:     if (parsedRecord.type === 'event' && parsedRecord.event === 'dispatch_failure') {
597:       return {
598:         ok: false,
599:         reason: 'dispatch_failure_logged',
600:         details:
601:           typeof parsedRecord.reason === 'string'
602:             ? `dispatch_failure:${parsedRecord.reason}${typeof parsedRecord.detail === 'string' ? `:${parsedRecord.detail}` : ''}`
603:             : 'dispatch_failure',
604:       };
605:     }
606: 
607:     if (!existsSync(input.iterationFile)) {
608:       return { ok: false, reason: 'iteration_file_missing', details: input.iterationFile };
609:     }
610: 
611:     if (statSync(input.iterationFile).size === 0) {
612:       return { ok: false, reason: 'iteration_file_empty', details: input.iterationFile };
613:     }
614: 
615:     if (parsedRecord.type !== CANONICAL_ITERATION_TYPE) {
616:       return {
617:         ok: false,
618:         reason: 'jsonl_wrong_type',
619:         details: `last record uses type='${String(parsedRecord.type)}' (expected '${CANONICAL_ITERATION_TYPE}')`,
620:       };
621:     }
622: 
623:     const requiredFields = new Set([
624:       ...input.requiredJsonlFields,
625:       ...(
626:         input.requiredJsonlFields.includes('findingsSummary') || input.requiredJsonlFields.includes('filesReviewed')
627:           ? REVIEW_ITERATION_FIELDS
628:           : []
629:       ),
630:     ]);
631:     const missingFields = [...requiredFields].filter((field) => !(field in parsedRecord));
632: 
633:     if (missingFields.length > 0) {
634:       return {
635:         ok: false,
636:         reason: 'jsonl_missing_fields',
637:         details: `missing: ${missingFields.join(',')}`,
638:       };
639:     }
640: 
641:     if (requiredFields.has('filesReviewed') && !Array.isArray(parsedRecord.filesReviewed)) {
642:       return {
643:         ok: false,
644:         reason: 'jsonl_missing_fields',
645:         details: 'filesReviewed must be an array of reviewed file paths',
646:       };
647:     }
648:     if (requiredFields.has('dimensions') && !Array.isArray(parsedRecord.dimensions)) {
649:       return {
650:         ok: false,
651:         reason: 'jsonl_missing_fields',
652:         details: 'dimensions must be an array',
653:       };
654:     }
655:     if (requiredFields.has('findingDetails') && !Array.isArray(parsedRecord.findingDetails)) {
656:       return {
657:         ok: false,
658:         reason: 'jsonl_missing_fields',
659:         details: 'findingDetails must be an array',
660:       };
661:     }
662:     if (requiredFields.has('newFindingsRatio') && typeof parsedRecord.newFindingsRatio !== 'number') {
663:       return {
664:         ok: false,
665:         reason: 'jsonl_missing_fields',
666:         details: 'newFindingsRatio must be a number',
667:       };
668:     }
669: 
670:     if (input.executorKind && input.executorKind !== 'native' && !isObjectRecord(parsedRecord.executor)) {
671:       return {
672:         ok: false,
673:         reason: 'executor_missing',
674:         details: `missing executor provenance for non-native executor kind '${input.executorKind}'`,
675:       };
676:     }
677: 
678:     let deltaIterationRecord: Record<string, unknown> | null = null;
679:     if (input.deltaFilePath) {
680:       if (!existsSync(input.deltaFilePath)) {
681:         return { ok: false, reason: 'delta_file_missing', details: input.deltaFilePath };
682:       }
683:       if (statSync(input.deltaFilePath).size === 0) {
684:         return { ok: false, reason: 'delta_file_empty', details: input.deltaFilePath };
685:       }
686:       const deltaContent = readFileSync(input.deltaFilePath, 'utf8');
687:       deltaIterationRecord = findLastIterationRecord(deltaContent);
688:       if (!deltaIterationRecord) {
689:         return {
690:           ok: false,
691:           reason: 'delta_file_missing_iteration_record',
692:           details: `${input.deltaFilePath} has no record with type='${CANONICAL_ITERATION_TYPE}'`,
693:         };
694:       }
695:     }
696: 
697:     if (parsedRecord.reviewDepthSchemaVersion !== 2) {
698:       if (isLegacyNonTrivialReviewRecord(parsedRecord)) {
699:         warnings.push({
700:           code: 'legacy_unversioned_record',
701:           detail: 'non-trivial review record has no reviewDepthSchemaVersion: 2 discriminator; v2 depth checks skipped',
702:           fieldPath: 'reviewDepthSchemaVersion',
703:         });
704:       }
705:     } else {
706:       const enforcementMode = getV2EnforcementMode();
707:       if (enforcementMode === 'off') {
708:         if (Array.isArray(parsedRecord.searchLedger) && parsedRecord.searchLedger.length > 0) {
709:           warnings.push({
710:             code: 'ledger_present_but_unverified',
711:             detail: 'DEEP_REVIEW_V2_ENFORCEMENT=off skipped v2 searchLedger validation',
712:             fieldPath: 'searchLedger',
713:           });
714:         }
715:       } else {
716:         const v2Failures = validateV2IterationRecord(parsedRecord, deltaIterationRecord);
717:         const identityFailure = v2Failures.find((failure) => failure.reason === 'delta_iteration_id_mismatch');
718:         if (identityFailure) {
719:           return {
720:             ok: false,
721:             reason: identityFailure.reason,
722:             details: identityFailure.details,
723:             warnings: warnings.length > 0 ? warnings : undefined,
724:           };
725:         }
726:         if (v2Failures.length > 0 && enforcementMode === 'strict') {
727:           return {
728:             ok: false,
729:             reason: v2Failures[0].reason,
730:             details: v2Failures[0].details,
731:             warnings: warnings.length > 0 ? warnings : undefined,
732:           };
733:         }
734:         if (v2Failures.length > 0) {
735:           warnings.push({
736:             code: 'applicability_strict_unenforced',
737:             detail: 'DEEP_REVIEW_V2_ENFORCEMENT=warn converted v2 depth failures to advisories',
738:             fieldPath: 'reviewDepthApplicability.enforcement',
739:           });
740:           warnings.push(...warningsFromV2Failures(v2Failures));
741:         }
742:       }
743:     }
744: 
745:     // Optional evidence contract: malformed metadata warns, absent metadata is
746:     // silent, and the verdict stays ok:true so a legacy exchange that omits the
747:     // metadata still passes. No new blocking failure reason is introduced; the
748:     // strict opt-in marks the advisory without rejecting the exchange.
749:     if (getEvidenceEnforcementMode() !== 'off') {
750:       const evidenceWarnings = evidenceAdvisories(parsedRecord);
751:       if (evidenceWarnings.length > 0) {
752:         if (getEvidenceEnforcementMode() === 'strict') {
753:           warnings.push({
754:             code: 'evidence_contract_strict_unenforced',
755:             detail: 'DEEP_LOOP_EVIDENCE_ENFORCEMENT=strict surfaced malformed evidence as advisories; no blocking reason is defined yet',
756:             fieldPath: 'evidence',
757:           });
758:         }
759:         warnings.push(...evidenceWarnings);
760:       }
761:     }
762: 
763:     const verificationResult = runOptionalVerificationPass(input.iterationFile, input.recipeConfig);
764:     if (!verificationResult.ok) {
765:       appendJsonlRecord(
766:         input.stateLogPath,
767:         JSON.parse(buildVerificationDegradedEvent({
768:           confidence: verificationResult.confidence,
769:           threshold: verificationResult.threshold,
770:           language: verificationResult.language,
771:           details: verificationResult.details,
772:         })),
773:       );
774:       return {
775:         ok: false,
776:         reason: 'verification_degraded',
777:         details: verificationResult.details,
778:       };
779:     }
780:   } catch (error: unknown) {
781:     const details = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
782:     return { ok: false, reason: 'jsonl_parse_error', details };
783:   }
784: 
785:   try {
786:     warnings.push(...computeBehavioralAdvisories(readFileSync(input.iterationFile, 'utf8')));
787:   } catch {
788:     // behavioral advisory is best-effort and verdict-neutral; never fail on it
789:   }
790: 
791:   return warnings.length > 0 ? { ok: true, warnings } : { ok: true };
792: }
793: 
794: /**
795:  * Validate iteration outputs and throw on failure.
796:  *
797:  * @param input - Validation input with paths, field requirements, and config.
798:  * @throws {@link PostDispatchValidationError} If validation fails.
799:  */
800: export function validateOrThrow(input: PostDispatchValidateInput): void {
801:   const result = validateIterationOutputs(input);
802: 
803:   if (!result.ok) {
804:     throw new PostDispatchValidationError(result);
805:   }
806: }
